sn45.26:2.2: “Wrong knowledge” and “wrong freedom” and their “right” equivalents are sometimes added to the eightfold path to make ten (eg. DN 18:27.8, MN 117:35.13, AN 11.10:4.3). They are not formally defined, but it would seem that “right knowledge” is the understanding that arises when all eight path factors are complete, namely the knowledge of stream-entry; while “right freedom” is the liberation of the perfected one (however, see SN 55.26 for a counterexample). “Wrong knowledge” and “wrong freedom” fall short of that, and in particular would include cases of people who falsely believe they are enlightened and liberated.
